questions.q: Which 1957 epic film, directed by Mehboob Khan, is considered his most celebrated work?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan directed Mother India (1957), widely regarded as his masterpiece and one of Indian cinema's most iconic films.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's film Mother India earned India its first nomination for which major international award?
options: The Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film
questions.explanation: Mother India (1957) was India's first film nominated for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film.

questions.q: What was the name of the film production company founded by Mehboob Khan?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan founded Mehboob Productions, under which he made his major large-scale dramas.

questions.q: Which actress played the lead role of Radha in Mehboob Khan's Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Nargis played the central role of Radha, the suffering mother, in Mehboob Khan's Mother India.

questions.q: In which year was Mehboob Khan's landmark film Mother India released?
questions.explanation: Mother India was released in 1957.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan was primarily known for working in which film industry?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan was a prominent director-producer of Hindi cinema (Bollywood).

questions.q: Which 1949 Mehboob Khan film starred Dilip Kumar, Raj Kapoor and Nargis in a love triangle?
questions.explanation: Andaz (1949) featured Dilip Kumar, Raj Kapoor and Nargis in a famous love-triangle drama.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's 1952 Technicolor adventure film was titled what?
questions.explanation: Aan (1952) was Mehboob Khan's grand colour adventure film, promoted as India's first film in Technicolor.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan worked mainly as which of the following in the film industry?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan was a noted film director and producer.

questions.q: Mother India, directed by Mehboob Khan, centres on the struggles of which central figure?
questions.explanation: Mother India tells the story of a poverty-stricken peasant mother who raises her sons through hardship.

questions.q: In which year was Mehboob Khan born?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan was born on 9 September 1907 in Bilimora, in present-day Gujarat.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's 1940 film Aurat (Woman) was a precursor to which later film of his?
questions.explanation: Aurat (1940) told an earlier version of the same Radha story that Mehboob Khan later reworked as Mother India (1957).

questions.q: Which actor played the rebellious son Birju in Mehboob Khan's Mother India?
questions.explanation: Sunil Dutt played Birju, the rebellious younger son, in Mother India.

questions.q: Who composed the music for Mehboob Khan's Mother India (1957)?
questions.explanation: Naushad composed the celebrated music for Mother India, continuing his long association with Mehboob Khan.

questions.q: The logo of Mehboob Productions famously featured what imagery?
options: A hammer and sickle with a couplet
questions.explanation: The Mehboob Productions logo showed a hammer and sickle accompanied by an Urdu couplet, reflecting its socialist leanings.

questions.q: In which year did Mehboob Khan die?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan died of a heart attack on 28 May 1964.

questions.q: Which composer collaborated with Mehboob Khan on hit films such as Anmol Ghadi (1946) and Andaz (1949)?
questions.explanation: Naushad was Mehboob Khan's regular music director, scoring films including Anmol Ghadi and Andaz.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's 1946 musical Anmol Ghadi featured which legendary singer-actress as its female lead before she moved to Pakistan?
questions.explanation: Anmol Ghadi (1946) starred singer-actress Noor Jehan as the female lead; she relocated to Pakistan after Partition.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan began his film career working in which capacity before becoming a director?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan started out as an extra and bit-part actor in the silent era, at Ardeshir Irani's Imperial Film Company, before rising to direction.

questions.q: In Mother India, the famous song 'Duniya Mein Hum Aaye Hain' was led by which playback singer, voicing Nargis?
questions.explanation: Lata Mangeshkar led 'Duniya Mein Hum Aaye Hain' (with her sisters Usha and Meena) in Naushad's score for Mother India, providing playback for Nargis.

questions.q: Before founding his own company, Mehboob Khan directed films for which major Bombay studio in the 1930s?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan worked as a director at Sagar Movietone in the 1930s before establishing Mehboob Productions.

questions.q: Which film studio did Mehboob Khan establish in Bandra, Bombay, named after himself?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan built Mehboob Studio on Hill Road in Bandra, which remains a well-known production facility in Mumbai.

questions.q: Which film did Mehboob Khan direct in 1954, starring Dilip Kumar, Madhubala and Nimmi in a story exploring guilt and morality?
questions.explanation: Amar (1954) was Mehboob Khan's psychological drama starring Dilip Kumar, Madhubala and Nimmi, scored by Naushad.

questions.q: In which town in Gujarat was Mehboob Khan born?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan was born in Bilimora, in present-day Gujarat.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's Mother India was famously beaten for the Academy Award for Best Foreign Language Film by which film?
questions.explanation: Mother India lost the Academy Award to Federico Fellini's Italian film Nights of Cabiria, reportedly by a single vote.

questions.q: What was the title of the project Mehboob Khan was developing about a 16th-century Kashmiri poet-queen, left unmade at his death?
questions.explanation: At his death in 1964, Mehboob Khan was contemplating a film on Habba Khatoon, the 16th-century poet and queen of Kashmir, which remained unmade.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's 1935 directorial debut for Sagar Movietone was which film?
questions.explanation: Mehboob Khan made his directorial debut with Al Hilal, released in English as Judgement of Allah, in 1935.

questions.q: During the making of Mother India, lead actor Sunil Dutt reportedly rescued co-star Nargis from what danger on set?
questions.explanation: Sunil Dutt rescued Nargis from a fire that broke out during a Mother India shoot, suffering burns himself, and the two later married.

questions.q: Mehboob Khan's son Iqbal Khan was associated with continuing which part of his father's legacy?
questions.explanation: After Mehboob Khan's death, his family, including his son Iqbal Khan, ran Mehboob Studio and Mehboob Productions.

questions.q: Which 1948 Mehboob Khan romantic film starred Naseem Banu as a woman who loses her memory in a train accident, caught in a love triangle?
questions.explanation: Anokhi Ada (1948), directed by Mehboob Khan with music by Naushad, starred Naseem Banu as an amnesiac woman at the centre of a romantic triangle.
